title: rootless podman build on nfs root fails
body:
**Description**

I'm attempting to use rootless podman on a system that is NFS booted. Based on https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/rootless-podman-nfs I took the following setup steps:

1. Create ext4 drive for /var/lib/containers/storage
2. Create /var/lib/containers/$USER
3. Bind mount /var/lib/containers/tmp to /var/tmp 
4. Update /etc/containers/storage.conf with `graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage/system"`
5. Update /etc/containers/storage.conf with `rootless_storage_path = "/var/lib/containers/storage/$USER"`

I am able to build images as root but not rootless

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Start with NFS booted system
2. Ensure graphroot and rootless_storage_path and /var/tmp are *not* NFS mounts
3. Attempt to build an image


**Describe the results you received:**

```
podman build -f alpine/Dockerfile -t thelounge/thelounge:4.1.0-alpine --build-arg THELOUNGE_VERSION=4.1.0 alpine
STEP 1: FROM arm32v7/node:12-alpine
STEP 2: ENV NODE_ENV production
--> Using cache d1c3ecc31bd32ef7713427fdca15cba113af533b07349e2ddf669e976315f536
STEP 3: ENV THELOUNGE_HOME "/var/opt/thelounge"
--> Using cache c96dcec7f9ba25bcc2f2ef7808c3be629251ada0a3c497ecf0636d90e2cfa66d
STEP 4: VOLUME "${THELOUNGE_HOME}"
Error: error building at STEP "VOLUME "${THELOUNGE_HOME}"": error reading symbolic link to "/var/opt/thelounge": error getting symbolic links: error checking symlink for "/var": error ensuring volume path "/var" exists: mkdir /var: file exists
: exit status 1
make: *** [Makefile:10: alpine] Error 125
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
sudo podman build -f alpine/Dockerfile -t thelounge/thelounge:4.1.0-alpine --build-arg THELOUNGE_VERSION=4.1.0 alpine
STEP 1: FROM arm32v7/node:12-alpine
STEP 2: ENV NODE_ENV production
--> Using cache 1ce37f36f6c9a0ff9d8bbd47e1d8b240ceae496f595af5ac3a4ee47f4975383a
STEP 3: ENV THELOUNGE_HOME "/var/opt/thelounge"
--> Using cache e81a68b625e273c33dd69a4caed35f98bf9ceb1fbd08cd0aca183e99016f4dde
STEP 4: VOLUME "${THELOUNGE_HOME}"
--> 9775ef94ad2
STEP 5: ENV PORT 9000
--> b46b928a998
STEP 6: EXPOSE ${PORT}
--> b1ae96fedfd
STEP 7: ENTRYPOINT ["docker-entrypoint.sh"]
--> ccfa992e79a
STEP 8: CMD ["thelounge", "start"]
--> 4f3a5da6276
STEP 9: COPY docker-entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh
--> 0c5592e6a3a
STEP 10: ARG THELOUNGE_VERSION=4.1.0
--> 559106e121e
STEP 11: RUN yarn --non-interactive --frozen-lockfile global add thelounge@${THELOUNGE_VERSION} &&     yarn --non-interactive cache clean
yarn global v1.22.0
etc
```

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah/unknown 1.14.5~74 armhf
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
N/A
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
podman version 1.8.2
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
PRETTY_NAME="Raspbian GNU/Linux 10 (buster)"
NAME="Raspbian GNU/Linux"
VERSION_ID="10"
VERSION="10 (buster)"
VERSION_CODENAME=buster

```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux pumpkin 4.19.97-v7+ #1294 SMP Thu Jan 30 13:15:58 GMT 2020 armv7l GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `grep -v '^#' /etc/containers/storage.conf | uniq`:**

```
[storage]

driver = ""

runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage/system"

rootless_storage_path = "/var/lib/containers/storage/$USER"

[storage.options]

additionalimagestores = [
]

[storage.options.overlay]

mountopt = "nodev"

[storage.options.thinpool]

```

comments.body: @mheon Could be an issue with default volumes?
I assume that `volume_path` in libpod.conf might be pointing somewhere strange (seems like it might be pointed at `/var`?)

```
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.14.3
  CgroupVersion: v1
  Conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.14, commit: '
  Distribution:
    distribution: raspbian
    version: "10"
  IDMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1001
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 165536
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1001
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 165536
      size: 65536
  MemFree: 110686208
  MemTotal: 971059200
  OCIRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: 'cri-o-runc: /usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: arm
  cpus: 4
  eventlogger: file
  hostname: pumpkin
  kernel: 4.19.97-v7+
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    Executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    Package: 'slirp4netns: /usr/bin/slirp4netns'
    Version: |-
      slirp4netns version 0.4.3
      commit: unknown
  uptime: 26h 54m 47.31s (Approximately 1.08 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/josh/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: 'fuse-overlayfs: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs'
      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.4.1
        fuse-overlayfs: version 0.7.6
        FUSE library version 3.4.1
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.27
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage/josh
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 3
  RunRoot: /run/user/1001/containers
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/josh
```

there could be a dangling symlink in `/var/opt/thelounge`.

https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/imagebuildah/chroot_symlink_linux.go#L120-L125

`os.MkdirAll()` fails with `EEXIST` if there is a dangling symlink in any of its components.

Could you verify if there are no dangling symlinks in the `/var/opt/thelounge` path?

Is `/var/opt` on NFS?  I don't think volumes on NFS will work with rootless.
Well volumes on NFS will sort of work, I believe.  As long as a process does not attempt to chown a file or take advantage of any file system User Namespace capabilities.
`/var/opt` is on NFS and there is no `/var/opt/thelounge`
@jokajak Did you ever get any further with this?
I ended up aborting on this and switching to a k3s based deployment. I'd be happy to circle back and test more with a newer version if that would be helpful.
Since the reporter has moved on, closing this issue.
